name: Michael Henderson
abstract: Michael Earl Henderson (July 7, 1951 – July 19, 2022) was an American bass guitarist and vocalist. He was known for his work with Miles Davis in the early 1970s on early fusion albums such as Jack Johnson, Live-Evil, and Agharta, along with a series of his own R&B/soul hits and others featuring him on vocals, particularly the Norman Conn…

concerts.label: New Audiences Jazz Anthology Series: Miles Davis

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Liebman
about_quotes.evidence: I knew Jack. A couple – “Hi. How you doing?” [?] said, “Yeah, how you doing?” But everybody’s packing up... The thing that made Michael Henderson great, I thought, was that he was very simple and he laid it right down.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave Liebman — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jack DeJohnette
about_quotes.evidence: Then Michael Henderson came into the band because Miles really wanted to go into more of an electric funk direction... After that Michael Henderson joined the band
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack DeJohnette — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
